---
format:
  dashboard:
    embed-resources: false
    theme:
      - default
      - lib/custom.scss
    nav-buttons: []
    orientation: rows
params:
  result_path: ""
  render_ts: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Anchor knitr to the project root so subsequent chunks can `source("R/…")`
# and so params$result_path (an absolute path) resolves cleanly. See
# render_dashboard.R for why this is set explicitly rather than relied upon.
proj_root <- normalizePath("..", winslash = "/")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = proj_root)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(plotly); library(DT); library(htmltools)
})
for (f in c("utils.R", "data_layer.R", "portfolio_runner.R",
            "comparison_engine.R", "ai_interpret.R", "plot_helpers.R",
            "html_components.R")) {
  source(file.path(proj_root, "R", f), local = FALSE)
}

result <- readRDS(params$result_path)
render_ts <- if (nzchar(params$render_ts %||% "")) {
  params$render_ts
} else {
  format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
}

grade_ok     <- isTRUE(result$gradebook_ok)
trend_df     <- if (grade_ok) median_trend(result$grades_df) else tibble::tibble()
breakdown_df <- assessment_breakdown(result$grades_df)
```

---
title: "`r sprintf('%s — %s (%s)', result$course_code, result$course_name, result$cohort_label)`"
---

# {.sidebar}

```{r}
ts_names <- if (!is.null(result$tag_coverage) && length(result$tag_coverage) > 0) {
  pretty_tagset_name(names(result$tag_coverage[[1]] %||% list()))
} else character(0)

ui_sidebar(
  course_code = result$course_code,
  course_name = result$course_name,
  metadata = c(
    result$cohort_label,
    sprintf("%d periods", length(result$periods)),
    sprintf("Data through %s", format(as.Date(result$use_end_date))),
    sprintf("Rendered %s", render_ts)
  ),
  tagsets = ts_names
)
```

# Overview

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "At a glance"
tagList(
  ui_kpi_row(
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Overall median",
      value    = if (grade_ok) sprintf("%.1f", result$kpi$overall_median %||% NA_real_) else "—",
      subvalue = "Across loaded periods"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Students assessed",
      value    = fmt_num(result$kpi$total_students),
      subvalue = "Distinct IDs"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Periods covered",
      value    = fmt_num(result$kpi$periods_covered)
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Tag sets",
      value    = fmt_num(result$kpi$n_tag_sets),
      subvalue = sprintf("Across %d period%s with tag data",
                         result$kpi$periods_with_tags %||% 0L,
                         if ((result$kpi$periods_with_tags %||% 0L) == 1) "" else "s")
    )
  ),
  if (!grade_ok) {
    HTML(paste0(
      '<div class="cvm-banner warn"><strong>Gradebook data unavailable.</strong> ',
      'Assessment plots will show placeholders. Curriculum tag coverage ',
      'and cross-year comparisons are unaffected.</div>'
    ))
  } else {
    HTML(paste0(
      '<div class="cvm-banner success">',
      sprintf('Data loaded successfully — %d students across %d periods.',
              result$kpi$total_students %||% 0,
              result$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0),
      '</div>'
    ))
  }
)
```

# Assessment Performance

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Median grade trend (interactive)"
tagList(
  ui_section_header("Trend across periods"),
  if (nrow(trend_df) == 0) {
    tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472; padding:24px;",
           "No trend data available for this class.")
  } else {
    trend_plotly(trend_df, title = sprintf("Median grade trend — %s", result$course_code))
  }
)
```

```{r}
#| title: "Median by assessment"
if (!is.null(result$assessment_plots) && !is.null(result$assessment_plots$median)) {
  tagList(
    ui_section_header("From the original portfolio pipeline"),
    plotly::ggplotly(result$assessment_plots$median) |>
      plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE)
  )
} else {
  tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472; padding:24px;",
         "Median grade plot not available.")
}
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Counts below 70% / 80% / 90%"
if (!is.null(result$assessment_plots) && !is.null(result$assessment_plots$pct)) {
  tagList(
    ui_section_header("Percentile thresholds"),
    plotly::ggplotly(result$assessment_plots$pct) |>
      plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE)
  )
} else {
  tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472; padding:24px;",
         "Percentile plot not available.")
}
```

```{r}
#| title: "Grade distribution by period"
# IMPORTANT: distribution_plot() returns a bare ggplot. htmltools::tagList
# cannot coerce ggplot2's S7 objects (typeof == "object") and throws
# "cannot coerce type 'object' to vector of type 'character'". We convert
# via ggplotly() so the child is an htmlwidget that htmltools understands.
# As a bonus the histogram becomes interactive, matching the sibling plots.
tagList(
  ui_section_header("Distribution of individual scores"),
  if (grade_ok && nrow(result$grades_df) > 0) {
    plotly::ggplotly(distribution_plot(result$grades_df)) |>
      plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE)
  } else {
    tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472; padding:24px;", "No grade data available.")
  }
)
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Assessment breakdown"
tagList(
  ui_section_header("Per-assessment statistics"),
  DT::datatable(
    breakdown_df,
    rownames   = FALSE,
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options    = list(dom = "Bfrtip", buttons = c("csv", "excel"),
                      pageLength = 15, scrollX = TRUE)
  ) |>
    DT::formatRound(columns = c("mean", "median", "sd"), digits = 2)
)
```

# Curriculum Tag Coverage

```{r}
#| output: asis
# Emission strategy (see ALSO course_combined_dashboard.qmd):
#
# Inside an `output: asis` chunk, custom htmltools components (spans with
# inline styles, divs, chips, etc.) need to reach pandoc as RAW HTML. If we
# print(tag) the way print.shiny.tag does, pandoc re-parses the spans as
# markdown inline text and we see literal "<span style=...>" show up on the
# page. The bulletproof pandoc-native fix is to wrap every bare-tag emission
# in a `{=html}` raw-HTML fence — pandoc preserves whatever's between the
# fences verbatim, no re-parsing.
#
# Plotly/DT widgets continue to use print(); their knit_print methods handle
# dependency registration correctly and are NOT affected by this issue.
emit_html <- function(tag) {
  if (is.null(tag)) return(invisible())
  cat("\n```{=html}\n", as.character(tag), "\n```\n\n", sep = "")
}

tc <- result$tag_coverage
first_period_tc <- if (!is.null(tc) && length(tc) > 0) tc[[1]] else NULL

if (is.null(first_period_tc) || length(first_period_tc) == 0) {
  emit_html(HTML('<div class="cvm-banner info">No tag coverage data available for this course.</div>'))
} else {
  cat("## {.tabset}\n\n")
  for (ts in names(first_period_tc)) {
    pretty <- pretty_tagset_name(ts)
    cat(sprintf("### %s\n\n", pretty))

    dfs    <- lapply(tc, function(x) x[[ts]])
    merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null), dfs))
    s      <- tag_coverage_summary(merged)
    bar    <- tag_bar_plotly(merged, title = pretty)

    # Coverage hero strip — bare htmltools, fence-wrapped.
    emit_html(ui_coverage_strip(
      covered = s$covered,
      total   = s$total,
      pct     = s$pct,
      label   = sprintf("%s covered", tolower(pretty))
    ))

    # Original cvm.course.portfolio coverage chart — widget, plain print().
    if (!is.null(result$tagset_plots[[ts]])) {
      emit_html(ui_section_header("Coverage chart"))
      print(plotly::ggplotly(result$tagset_plots[[ts]]) |>
              plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE))
    }

    # Tag-by-tag bar chart — widget.
    emit_html(ui_section_header("Tag-by-tag counts"))
    print(bar$plot)

    # Deprecated-tags note — bare.
    emit_html(ui_deprecated_note(bar$deprecated_count))

    # Uncovered tag chips — bare.
    emit_html(ui_section_header(sprintf("Uncovered tags (%d)", length(s$uncovered))))
    emit_html(ui_tag_chips(s$uncovered))
  }
}
```

# Course Insights

```{r}
narr_path <- sub("\\.rds$", "_narrative.rds", params$result_path)
narrative <- if (file.exists(narr_path)) readRDS(narr_path) else NULL

if (is.null(narrative)) {
  HTML('<div class="cvm-banner info">Narrative not available for this render.</div>')
} else if (isFALSE(narrative$available %||% TRUE)) {
  HTML(paste0(
    '<div class="cvm-narrative"><h3>Course narrative not available</h3><p>',
    htmlEscape(narrative$narrative),
    '</p></div>'
  ))
} else {
  flags_ui <- if (!is.null(narrative$flags) && nrow(narrative$flags) > 0) {
    tagList(lapply(seq_len(nrow(narrative$flags)), function(i) {
      f <- narrative$flags[i, ]
      klass <- switch(f$severity,
                      high   = "cvm-flag red",
                      medium = "cvm-flag amber",
                      low    = "cvm-flag green",
                      "cvm-flag amber")
      tags$div(class = klass,
        tags$div(
          tags$div(class = "label", toupper(f$severity)),
          tags$div(class = "issue", f$issue),
          tags$div(class = "rec",   f$recommendation)
        )
      )
    }))
  } else NULL

  questions_ui <- if (length(narrative$questions) > 0) {
    tags$ol(class = "cvm-questions",
            lapply(narrative$questions, tags$li))
  } else NULL

  tagList(
    tags$div(class = "cvm-narrative", HTML(markdown_to_html(narrative$narrative))),
    if (!is.null(flags_ui))     tagList(ui_section_header("Areas of concern"),   flags_ui),
    if (!is.null(questions_ui)) tagList(ui_section_header("Questions to explore"), questions_ui),
    tags$p(
      style = "color:#5A6472; font-size:12px; margin-top:24px;",
      sprintf("Generated %s using %s.",
              fmt_ts(narrative$generated_at),
              narrative$model %||% "—")
    )
  )
}
```

# Downloads

```{r}
# params$result_path points at {course_dir}/.render_cache/{tag}_result.rds.
# The PPTX / zipped workbooks live one level up, in the course folder itself.
course_dir <- dirname(dirname(params$result_path))

# --- Export artifacts (PPTX, Excel zip) generated by --with-exports ---------
pptx_files <- list.files(course_dir, pattern = "\\.pptx$",          full.names = TRUE)
xlsx_files <- list.files(course_dir, pattern = "_workbooks\\.zip$", full.names = TRUE)

download_row <- function(label, abs_path, href_rel) {
  if (!file.exists(abs_path)) return(NULL)
  size <- fmt_bytes(file.info(abs_path)$size)
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-download",
    tags$div(class = "label", label),
    tags$a(href = href_rel, target = "_blank", basename(abs_path),
           download = basename(abs_path)),
    tags$span(class = "size", size)
  )
}

export_items <- tagList(
  if (length(pptx_files) > 0) download_row("PowerPoint",
                                           pptx_files[[1]],
                                           basename(pptx_files[[1]])),
  if (length(xlsx_files) > 0) download_row("Excel workbooks (zip)",
                                           xlsx_files[[1]],
                                           basename(xlsx_files[[1]]))
)
has_exports <- length(pptx_files) > 0 || length(xlsx_files) > 0

exports_block <- tagList(
  ui_section_header("Export artifacts"),
  if (has_exports) {
    tags$div(class = "cvm-downloads", export_items)
  } else {
    HTML('<div class="cvm-banner info">No export files found. Re-run <code>render_dashboard.R</code> with <code>--with-exports</code> to generate the PowerPoint and Excel workbooks.</div>')
  }
)

# --- Course surveys ---------------------------------------------------------
# surveys_dir returns outputs/{course_code}_{class_slug}/surveys/, which is
# a different parent folder from course_dir (see R/utils.R for the rationale).
# Compute a relative path from this HTML's location to that folder so the
# <a href=…> links work whether the dashboards are served from disk or Box.
s_dir_abs <- surveys_dir(result$course_code, result$cohort_label)

# Relative hop from course_dir -> surveys folder: up one level, then into
# "{safe_code}_{class_slug}/surveys/".
safe_code <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "", result$course_code)
class_slug_local <- cohort_slug(result$cohort_label)
surveys_rel_base <- file.path("..", sprintf("%s_%s", safe_code, class_slug_local), "surveys")

surveys_block <- if (!dir.exists(s_dir_abs)) {
  # Folder not yet created — tell the viewer when it will be.
  ready_on <- if (!is.null(result$max_period_finish_date) &&
                  !is.na(result$max_period_finish_date)) {
    format(as.Date(result$max_period_finish_date) + 2)
  } else {
    "(period end date unknown)"
  }
  tagList(
    ui_section_header("Course surveys"),
    HTML(sprintf(
      '<div class="cvm-banner info">Survey folder will be created 2 days after course end (%s).</div>',
      ready_on
    ))
  )
} else {
  pdfs <- list.files(s_dir_abs, pattern = "\\.pdf$", full.names = TRUE)
  if (length(pdfs) == 0) {
    tagList(
      ui_section_header("Course surveys"),
      HTML(sprintf(
        '<div class="cvm-banner info">Survey folder ready — drop PDF files here to make them available for download: <code>%s</code></div>',
        htmlEscape(sub("^.*/outputs/", "outputs/", s_dir_abs))
      ))
    )
  } else {
    rows <- lapply(pdfs, function(p) {
      download_row(
        label    = "Survey PDF",
        abs_path = p,
        href_rel = file.path(surveys_rel_base, basename(p))
      )
    })
    tagList(
      ui_section_header("Course surveys"),
      tags$div(class = "cvm-downloads", do.call(tagList, rows))
    )
  }
}

tagList(exports_block, surveys_block)
```
